title: Tetranthera geniculata
taxonomy: Tetranthera geniculata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flowers and fruits, which are typical of the Annonaceae family. Within the Annonaceae family, Tetranthera geniculata belongs to the genus Tetranthera. The exact classification of Tetranthera geniculata can be further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta. By understanding its classification, researchers can better understand the plant's evolution, relationships, and ecological role.

intro: Tetranthera geniculata is a plant species commonly known as the "Thai Angelica" or "Thai Wild Ginger", belonging to the family Apiaceae. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Tetranthera geniculata include compounds like coumarins, flavonoids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tetranthera geniculata is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or essential oil, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats.
